text:
In a quiet town hidden behind high hills, lived a cat named Luna. She had a white fur like a snowflake’s gentle whisper. Luna spent her days batting butterflies away from blooming violet flowers.

One autumn evening, as the sun surrendered to twilight, Luna heard rustling outside. Curiosity got the better of her. Through the window, she saw a family of chipmunks. They were collecting nuts, chirping cheerfully.

Luna approached them cautiously. The chipmunks froze, looking up at her with wide eyes full of surprise.

“No fear,” Luna meowed, stretching lazily.

The chipmunks shook off their fear and scampered off to their burrow.

The next spring, Luna was ready too. She hoed dandelions into little bouquet piles and waited. Inch by glittering inch, she watched as little chipmunk feet decorated her bounty.

As autumn returned, Luna enjoyed nibbling around her treasure trove of yellow flowers.

That winter,
